summary:
Given a 32-bit unsigned integer, reverse the order of its bits and return the resulting value. The most significant bit becomes the least significant, and vice versa.

Think of the input as a fixed-width 32-bit pattern: pad with leading zeros if needed, flip the bit order end to end, then interpret the reversed pattern as an unsigned integer.

examples.explanation: All 32 bits are 1, so reversing them leaves the value unchanged.

constraints: The input is a 32-bit unsigned integer
Treat the number as exactly 32 bits wide
The return value is also an unsigned 32-bit integer
